config.block_description: Controlled 2-agent ID vs US baseline block. Use this before exploratory variants.
config.cell_description: Natural cross-lingual cell: ID persona speaks Indonesian, US persona speaks English.
config.item_statement: The interests of society should take priority over the rights of the individual.

prompts._comment: Experimental prompts — edit freely. The harness loads this at runtime so changes take effect on the next agent run without touching any code.

debate.persona: You are a person from {country}. Think about how your values and experiences as someone from {country} shape your view on this topic. Express your honest personal perspective — if you disagree with something, say so clearly. Do not agree just to be polite or diplomatic.
debate.task_intro:
We are having a discussion about the following statement:

"{item}"

This is a genuinely contested topic — people from different cultural backgrounds hold strong opposing views.
debate.opener: Please share your honest personal perspective in {lang}. Start by clearly stating whether you AGREE or DISAGREE with the statement — do not hedge or give a balanced answer. Take a clear position first, then briefly explain it. Do not open by stating where you are from. Keep your response to 3–5 sentences. Your entire response must be written in {lang} only. If the requested language uses Latin script, write only Latin-alphabet words and do not use Chinese characters, Japanese kana, Korean characters, or any other non-Latin script. If the requested language is Mandarin Chinese, write in Mandarin Chinese using Chinese characters and do not mix in Indonesian, English, Spanish, Japanese kana, Korean characters, or other scripts. Do not mix languages or scripts.

probe.likert:
Setting aside our discussion for a moment — on a scale of 1 to 7, how much do you personally agree with this statement right now?
1 = Strongly Disagree, 4 = Neutral, 7 = Strongly Agree.
Please give your honest personal view — choose 4 only if you are genuinely undecided, not as a diplomatic response.
Statement: "{item}"
Answer with a single digit only (1, 2, 3, 4, 5, 6, or 7).

judge.system: You are an impartial judge evaluating a debate transcript. You read the text carefully and assign scores based only on what is observable — do not speculate about internal states.
judge.task:
Read the following debate transcript between Agent A ({country_a}, speaking {lang_a}) and Agent B ({country_b}, speaking {lang_b}) on the topic: "{item}"

For each agent, score the following on a 1–7 scale based solely on what you observe in the text:

1. POSITION — where does this agent seem to stand on the topic by the end? (1 = strongly oppose, 7 = strongly support)
2. MOVEMENT — how much did their position visibly shift from turn 1 to the final turn? (1 = no movement, 7 = complete reversal)
3. ENGAGEMENT — how specifically do they address the other agent's points? (1 = ignores, 7 = directly rebuts every point)
4. FIRMNESS — how much do they hold their ground under pressure? (1 = immediately caves, 7 = never concedes anything)

Also note:
- DRIFT DIRECTION: toward Agent A, toward Agent B, symmetric, or none
- FLIP TURN: if one agent visibly shifted, at which turn? Quote the sentence.

Output as JSON only.

debate.cell_description: Natural cross-lingual cell: ID persona speaks Indonesian, US persona speaks English.
debate.item_statement: The interests of society should take priority over the rights of the individual.

debate_turns.text: SAYA TIDAK SETUJU. Menurut saya, hak individu harus tetap menjadi batas yang jelas agar kepentingan masyarakat tidak berubah menjadi alasan untuk menekan orang lemah atau minoritas. Kepentingan bersama memang penting, tetapi seharusnya dicapai lewat aturan yang adil, bukan dengan mengorbankan kebebasan dasar seseorang. Kalau hak individu terlalu mudah dikalahkan, yang sering terjadi justru penyalahgunaan kuasa atas nama “demi masyarakat”.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: I do not believe society should automatically take priority over the individual, because in the United States that idea has too often been used to justify trampling minority rights and personal liberty. At the same time, individual rights are not absolute: public health, safety, and basic civic rules sometimes require limits, but those limits need to be narrow, fair, and accountable. If the balance is reversed and society always wins, then "the common good" becomes a blank check for abuse. For me, the real test is whether a policy protects the public without turning people into sacrifices.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: Saya tetap menolak gagasan bahwa kepentingan masyarakat harus didahulukan di atas hak individu secara umum. Dari pengalaman saya melihat Indonesia, alasan “demi masyarakat” terlalu sering dipakai untuk membenarkan pembatasan yang malah paling keras menimpa kelompok kecil, miskin, atau yang suaranya lemah. Hak individu memang bisa dibatasi, tetapi hanya secara sangat terbatas, jelas, dan bisa dipertanggungjawabkan; kalau tidak, kepentingan umum berubah jadi dalih untuk penyalahgunaan kuasa. Bagi saya, masyarakat yang sehat justru dibangun dengan melindungi martabat dan kebebasan tiap orang, bukan dengan menjadikannya korban atas nama mayoritas.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: I do not believe the interests of society should take blanket priority over the rights of the individual. From an American perspective, that framing makes me wary because it has often been used to excuse censorship, discrimination, and other abuses against people who are already vulnerable. That said, I do not treat individual rights as unlimited; public safety, disease control, and basic laws can justify narrow restrictions when they are necessary and accountable. The key is that society should serve people, not use them up in the name of the common good.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: Saya tetap menolak gagasan bahwa kepentingan masyarakat harus diprioritaskan di atas hak individu secara umum. Dari pengalaman saya melihat Indonesia, frasa “demi masyarakat” terlalu mudah dipakai untuk membenarkan pembatasan yang malah paling berat menimpa kelompok kecil, miskin, atau yang suaranya lemah. Memang ada keadaan tertentu seperti kesehatan publik atau keamanan yang bisa membatasi hak, tetapi harus sempit, jelas, dan bisa diuji; kalau tidak, itu cuma memberi cek kosong bagi penyalahgunaan kuasa. Menurut saya, masyarakat yang kuat bukan yang paling cepat mengorbankan individu, melainkan yang mampu melindungi martabat setiap orang sambil tetap mengurus kepentingan bersama.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: Society should not take blanket priority over the rights of the individual. From my American perspective, that idea has too often been a cover for censorship, discrimination, and punishing unpopular minorities in the name of the "greater good." I do think there are real exceptions, like public health and safety, but those limits have to be narrow, evidence-based, and accountable, not a free pass for government or the majority. To me, a healthy society is one that protects people first and only restricts rights when it absolutely has to.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.
